mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth reduces recession risk and boosts corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in GDP growth quarters are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following rate hikes > 0.25pp are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate labor market deterioration, which forecasts weaker consumer demand and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals robust manufacturing and wholesale activity, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in rising INDPRO periods are not significantly positive when CPIAUCSL is below 3.0% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly housing starts decline exceeding 10% signals weakening construction demand and broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following housing starts drops > 10% are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation readings combined with elevated federal funds rates indicate a restrictive monetary stance failing to cool prices, which pressures equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high CPI/high FEDFUNDS regimes are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings balances reflect stronger household balance sheets, which support consumer spending and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in rising RSAFS periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 5.0%, it signals a labor market inflection point that historically precedes equity drawdowns and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE breaches 5.0% are not significantly negative.
